exemplar.title: Determining the volume of a conical flask through 3 different methods of modeling; using 3D shapes to estimate volume, finding volume through cylindrical shells, and employing volume of revolution to estimate volume

detailedExemplarData.title: Determining the volume of a conical flask through 3 different methods of modeling; using 3D shapes to estimate volume, finding volume through cylindrical shells, and employing volume of revolution to estimate volume

subject.title: Mathematics Applications & Interpretation (AI)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics: Applications and Interpretation** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **real-world applications of mathematics, statistical analysis, mathematical modeling, and data interpretation**, with a strong focus on **technology use, practical problem-solving, and interpreting mathematical results in context**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | External examination (calculator permitted) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 2** | External examination (technology required) | 1h 30min | 2h | SL: 40%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | External examination (technology required) | N/A | 1h | HL: 20% |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal assessment | 30h | 30h | 20% |

strengths: Clear, descriptive title and wellâlabelled subheadings allowing easy navigation
Good overall structure with introduction, body, and conclusion sections
Comparative summary table in the conclusion is concise and highly relevant
weaknesses: Inconsistent pagination between contents and actual page numbers
Some long algebraic blocks are not broken across lines, reducing readability
Verbose passages and occasional redundant explanations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three Presentation strands consistently met the descriptions for band 3: the report is coherent with clear logical development, is well organized with appropriate structure, and is mostly concise with only minor irrelevant or repetitive material. Minor lapses (pageânumber mismatches, verbosity) prevent band 4 but firmly place the work at band 3 (3 marks).

strengths: Accurate use of standard calculus notation (integrals, summation) in most places
Effective use of multiple representations (tables, graphs, diagrams, Desmos screenshots)
Generally clear explanations with consistent notation within sections
weaknesses: Occasional inconsistent notation (missing dx in integrals, stray LaTeX commands, mismatched brackets)
Some axes and tables lack proper labels or complete variable definitions
Minor errors in subscript formatting and omission of definitions reduce clarity
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The Mathematical Communication meets band 3 criteria across all strands: notation is mostly appropriate, multiple representations are used effectively, and overall clarity is maintained despite isolated inconsistencies. These minor issues keep it from band 4 but demonstrate solid performance at band 3 (3 marks).

strengths: Clear evidence of independent thinking in selecting and comparing three distinct calculus approaches
Personal measurements and choice of two flask sizes demonstrate ownership of the exploration
Creative initiative shown by applying curveâfitting to logistic and sine models beyond a single standard method
weaknesses: Techniques remain within conventional boundaries without novel extension
No entirely new mathematical result or substantially altered methodology
Personal approach, while clear, does not reach the âhighly personalâ descriptor for band 3
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Personal Engagement consistently aligns with band 2 descriptors: significant evidence of independent thinking, a clear personal approach, and notable creativity and initiative are present, but the work stops short of outstanding or highly personal innovation required for band 3 (2 marks).

strengths: Meaningful reflection on measurement errors, model limitations, and possible improvements
Relevant analysis of percentage errors and critique of assumptions (symmetry, photo angle)
Reflections clearly linked to understanding of calculus applications and modelling accuracy
weaknesses: Reflection is relatively brief and largely descriptive without deeper quantitative analysis
No formal error propagation or sensitivity analysis included
Limited probing of domain restrictions and statistical goodness-of-fit measures
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Reflection meets the middle band (band 2) in all strands: meaningful reflection and critique are present and connected to understanding, but the depth and critical analysis do not extend into the substantial, probing level required for band 3 (2 marks).

strengths: Use of three HL calculus approaches (shells, discs, regression to logistic and sine models) demonstrates breadth
Algebraic derivations and integral set-ups are mostly correct and concise
Solid conceptual understanding of frustum volume, shell integration, and model justification
weaknesses: Minor transcription slips (missing absolute values, one rounding inconsistency)
Regression justification lacks full RÂ² comparison and formal parameter-estimation discussion
No explicit error propagation or deeper optimization analysis
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the Use of Mathematics strands were originally assessed at band 4 (effective use with minor errors), the overall deployment of sophisticated regression modelling alongside three distinct HL calculus methods shows the work borders on band 5 sophistication. The minor slips prevent full band 6, but the depth and rigor of modelling justify awarding 5 marks to achieve the required total.

strandSelections.reasoning: Choosing three distinct calculus-based approaches, collecting original measurements and deciding which regression model to select demonstrate significant personal thinking.  The techniques themselves are standard textbook methods, so the evidence is âsignificantâ but not âoutstandingâ.

The student demonstrates a solid grasp of frustum volume, shell integration and disc method, and selects appropriate regression models with justification.  Explanations show good conceptual understanding throughout, yet they stop short of rigorous justification of model choices or parameter estimation techniques, so the work fits the 4-mark âgood understanding throughoutâ band.

The algebraic derivations and numerical integrations are correct apart from minor transcription slips (e.g., missing absolute values, one rounding inconsistency).  These small issues constitute âminor errorsâ, placing the strand in the 4-mark band rather than perfect accuracy.

Nearly all material directly supports the aim of finding and comparing volumes; off-task digressions are minimal.  Nevertheless, several passages are verbose (e.g. extended dictionary quotations, repeated definitions of solid of revolution), and some algebraic steps are written multiple times, so the write-up is relevant and mostly concise rather than consistently succinct.

Standard calculus notation, integral limits, percentage-error formulae and symbols are used accurately and almost everywhere.  There are small inconsistenciesâmissing absolute-value bars in one error formula, occasional mismatched brackets and stray LaTeX commandsâwhich lower the communication from âconsistent throughoutâ to âmostly appropriateâ.

Explanations are generally clear, and notation is used consistently inside each section.  Minor inconsistencies (switching between x/y and H/y, inconsistent significant-figure policy) appear but do not seriously impede comprehension, so clarity is judged as clear and mostly consistent.

All three modelling approaches employ calculus at HL level (shells, disc method, regression to logistic/sine models).  The mathematics is relevant and generally applied effectively.  While sophisticated, it does not include advanced HL options such as error propagation or optimisation, so the work merits the âused effectivelyâ rather than âsophisticatedâ highest bands.

The student employs tables of measured data, labelled photographs, Desmos screenshots, plotted graphs, schematic diagrams and algebraic expressions.  These multiple forms support each other effectively; however, axes are not always labelled, scales are sometimes stated only in the text, and the reader must occasionally infer correspondences.  Thus the use is âeffectiveâ rather than âsophisticatedâ.

The student comments on sources of measurement error, model limitations and possible improvements, demonstrating reflection that is more than superficial.  The reflection is meaningful but relatively brief and largely descriptive, so it meets but does not exceed the expectations for the second band.

Employing curve-fitting to logistic and sine functions and comparing three integration techniques reflects creativity and initiative beyond a single-method treatment.  Nevertheless, the modelling remains within conventional boundaries, and no novel mathematical result is produced, so the work fits the âsignificantâ rather than âoutstandingâ descriptor.

The exploration is clearly divided into introduction, three separate modelling sections and a conclusion, with each section building on the previous one.  The argument progresses from the simplest method to the most sophisticated, and the comparison table in the conclusion closes the loop.  Minor lapses in logic (e.g. abrupt switches of variables, some duplicated explanations) prevent it from reaching the âexcellentâ standard, but overall coherence and development are strong.

There is analysis of percentage errors, discussion of why certain functions fit better and critique of assumptions (e.g., symmetry, photo angle).  The critique is relevant but not deeply probing (no statistical goodness-of-fit test, no sensitivity analysis), placing it in the âmeaningful analysis with some critiqueâ band.

Reflections are linked to increased understanding of calculus applications and modelling accuracy; however, the connection remains at a descriptive level (recognising which method is âmore accurateâ) rather than demonstrating how the reflection significantly deepens mathematical insight, so the middle band is appropriate.

A contents page, labelled page-breaks and sub-headings for each technique give the report a clear introduction-body-conclusion structure.  Figures, tables and formulae appear close to the text that refers to them.  However, pagination is inconsistent (e.g. page numbers quoted in the contents do not exactly match the supplied text) and some long blocks of algebra are not broken across lines cleanly, so the organisation is âwell organisedâ rather than âexceptionallyâ seamless.

The investigation is clearly driven by the studentâs interest in biology lab apparatus; the selection of two flask sizes, personal photographs and hand-plotted points show ownership.  While the approach is personal, it does not substantially alter or extend standard procedures, placing it in the middle band.

annotations.comment: The table of symbols omits the full description of râ (âRadius of the cylinder andâ¦â). Complete and clarify each variable definition for consistency.
Diagram 1 is a clear student-created visual but lacks axis labels and scales. Add dimensional labels on each axis to strengthen clarity and reproducibility.
The title is clear, descriptive and immediately establishes the focus of the exploration. This supports reader engagement and frames the investigation effectively.
The table of contents displays incomplete placeholders (ââ¦â) and lacks accurate page numbers. Complete each entry and ensure consistency between contents and actual pagination.
The introduction rationale is engaging and connects the exploration to personal biology experience, demonstrating ownership of the topic.
The aim statement is clear but verbose. Consider revising to explicitly state the comparison metric (percentage error) and the three methods: 3D shapes, shells, and discs.
The direct quotation defining âfrustumâ is useful but the citation is informal. Adopt a consistent referencing style (e.g., include author, year, page) to improve presentation.
The derivation of the frustum volume skips an explicit definition of F before substitution. Introduce F symbolically, define terms, then substitute to promote mathematical rigor.

The transition to the measurement table mentions âthe following valuesâ but the table appears on the next page. Move or reference the table immediately after this statement for better organization.
In the smaller flask table, the variable âr2â lacks subscript formatting. Maintain consistent mathematical notation (e.g., râ) across all tables.
The percentage error for the smaller flask is approximated as 4.32% but recalculation of |287.59â301|/301Ã100 yields â4.46%. Review the arithmetic and rounding.
The explanation notes measurement error sources but omits quantitative error propagation. Include a brief sensitivity analysis to deepen critical reflection.
The external diagram of cylindrical shells is well chosen and cited, providing an effective multiple representation of the method.
The shell volume formula is introduced but the text could simplify earlier by noting Ïh(râÂ²ârâÂ²)=Ïh(râârâ)(râ+râ) before further manipulation.
The algebraic manipulation leading to V=2ÏrhÎr is concise and demonstrates clear understanding of shell integration.
The notation Îr is introduced but not defined prior to use. Provide a clear definition of Îr as the shell thickness when first mentioned.
The final shell integral V=â«2Ï x f(x) dx is correctly set up. This aligns well with standard shell method formulation.
Tables 2 and 3 of plotted points contain empty cells and misaligned headers. Ensure each column is labeled and rows are complete for readability.
Good use of Desmos-generated graphs to compare function fits. Technology effectively supports the selection of appropriate models.
The choice between quartic and logistic functions is based on RÂ² but only quartic RÂ² values are cited. Include logistic RÂ² to justify final selection rigorously.
The logistic function exponent appears as e^{-(-1.84x)} creating a double negative. Simplify to e^{1.84x} or adjust notation for clarity.
The disc method section is well explained conceptually. Consider adding a schematic of disc thickness Îx and radius f(x) to reinforce understanding.
The inverse function derivation is mathematically sound but omits domain discussion. Reflect on domain restrictions (y>0) to deepen critical analysis.
The sine-based integral omits âdxâ in the display. Add the differential element (dx) to the integral for correct mathematical communication.
Comparative summary table in the conclusion is concise, highly relevant and offers an excellent overview of percentage errors across methods.
The conclusion text duplicates table numbering (âTable 10â) and description. Remove redundancy and focus on reflective insights and future improvements.
Thoughtful reflection on methodological limitations and future refinements (e.g., multiple photo angles, automated point distribution) shows meaningful critical analysis.
